findings.deletionTest: "I can see the first three steps."
findings.reason: Pattern 2 fragment pivot; the next sentence already carries the real limitation without the negation scaffold.
findings.suggestedFix: "I can give you the first three steps."

findings.subject: Bread
findings.realAgent: The plague reaches people because everyone eats bread at the table.
findings.reason: The sentence launders the delivery mechanism onto bread instead of naming the human habit and route that make the cruelty intimate.
findings.suggestedFix: The cruelty reached people through bread.

findings.text: Orders moving fast enough that nobody repeated them.
findings.subject: Orders
findings.realAgent: Runners and officers are relaying commands fast enough that no one needs to repeat an order.
findings.reason: The action belongs to the people carrying and relaying commands, not to the orders themselves.
findings.suggestedFix: Runners and officers were moving fast enough that nobody repeated an order.

findings.text: It only made the refusal gathering in the room feel more exact.
findings.subject: the refusal
findings.realAgent: Arthas's reactions and reading pattern make his coming refusal clearer to Jaina.
findings.reason: The sentence turns Arthas's imminent refusal into a self-gathering abstraction instead of naming the person tightening into that decision.
findings.suggestedFix: It only made Arthas's refusal feel more exact.

findings.text: Command does that in the field.
findings.subject: Command
findings.realAgent: Field commanders sort one threat to the healers and another to the soldiers.
findings.reason: The line hands human triage behavior to an abstraction and blurs the actual decision-makers.
findings.suggestedFix: Commanders do that in the field.

findings.text: Jaina had gone in believing a clean argument, laid out cleanly, would force the room to yield.
findings.subject: the room
findings.realAgent: Arthas is the one Jaina hoped would yield to the argument.
findings.reason: The sentence gives surrender to the room instead of naming the person whose refusal actually matters.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina had gone in believing a clean argument, laid out cleanly, would force Arthas to yield.

findings.subject: It
findings.realAgent: The taint is coming in with each breath Jaina draws near the city.
findings.reason: The line gives the corruption a decorative locomotion verb instead of naming the concrete carrier.
findings.suggestedFix: It came in with every breath.

findings.text: The towers were full and feeding the road.
findings.subject: The towers
findings.realAgent: Full granary towers and active wagon traffic are still sending grain out onto the road.
findings.reason: The sentence launders the grain movement onto the towers instead of naming the active wagon-and-loading flow.
findings.suggestedFix: The towers were full, and wagons were still pulling grain out toward the road.

findings.text: A pressure that had soaked into the place so thoroughly it moved with the ordinary labor of it: flour in sacks, ledgers, bakers, carts, clerks, all of it doing the day's work over a center that should have sent every bell in Lordaeron ringing.
findings.subject: A pressure
findings.realAgent: The city's ordinary labor—flour sacks, ledgers, bakers, carts, and clerks—is carrying contamination through the workday.
findings.reason: The line names the concrete carriers and still lets an abstraction do the moving, which is exactly the laundering this guide hunts.
findings.suggestedFix: The pressure had soaked so deeply into the place that flour in sacks, ledgers, bakers, carts, and clerks all carried it through the day's work.

findings.text: A camp would wait for orders.
findings.subject: A camp
findings.realAgent: Soldiers in camp would wait for orders to reach them.
findings.reason: The sentence compresses a human chain of obedience into an abstract camp noun and drops the people actually waiting.
findings.suggestedFix: Soldiers in camp would wait for orders.

findings.text: The wheel-inside-wheel mark on eleven depots.
findings.reason: This is one of the excess 'mark/marked' uses beyond the chapter cap, and stronger physical instances survive elsewhere.
findings.suggestedFix: The wheel-inside-wheel sigil on eleven depots.

findings.text: She knew every mark on it.
findings.reason: This is a generic mental-use instance of 'mark' and is weaker than the chapter's literal sign references.
findings.suggestedFix: She knew every notation on it.

findings.text: The marked depots, the cleaned shelves, the blank books, the dead man at Millbrook Spur without a wound, the Barov name appearing too often to ignore and still not often enough to prove.
findings.reason: The adjective is functional but expendable, and the chapter has already spent its cap on stronger physical sign uses.
findings.suggestedFix: The tagged depots, the cleaned shelves, the blank books, the dead man at Millbrook Spur without a wound, the Barov name appearing too often to ignore and still not often enough to prove.

findings.text: Two men on two roads using the same farewell was enough to mark it.
findings.reason: This is the weakest mental-note instance and becomes obvious repetition after the earlier physical mark language.
findings.suggestedFix: Two men on two roads using the same farewell was enough to flag it.

findings.text: Every depot they had traced, every mark copied from a ledger, every village that had sickened without understanding why—all of it pointed here, and here was a functioning node in plain sight.
findings.reason: This late-chapter reuse pushes the 'mark' family well past cap and is weaker than the earlier literal sign references kept.
findings.suggestedFix: Every depot they had traced, every symbol copied from a ledger, every village that had sickened without understanding why—all of it pointed here, and here was a functioning node in plain sight.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: 4 moderate intent-smear kills remain in command/room/pressure/camp abstractions, plus 11 mild kills from one negation pivot and an overloaded 'mark' tic cluster.

textureMetrics.interpretation: PARTICIPIAL PHRASES: 2.1% (floor: 5.0%, human: 11.0%). Add ', Ving' phrases to connect actions: ', turning back to the fire' / ', gripping the railing with both hands'. These create connective tissue between actions.
